english: return? lóg-pa (log pa) ºtad ('thad) Glr., lóg-la (log la) ºdód-do ('dod do) Glr. let us turn back, pýir (phyir) lóg-pai (log pa'i) lam (lam) the way back. — 2. to come back, to come again. — 3. to turn round, to be turned upside down, to tumble down W., e.g. of a pile of wood etc.; ṅo (ngo) lóg-pa (log pa) or llóg-pa (llog pa) to turn away one's face, always used fig. for to turn one's back on, to apostatize ºkór-bai ('khor ba'i) yul (yul) ṅo-llóg-na (ngo llog na) if you mean to turn your back to the land of the cycle of existences, more frq.: lóg-pa (log pa) byéd-pa (byed pa) to revolt, to rebel, lóg-pa (log pa) rtsóm-pa (rtsom pa) to plot, to stir up, an insurrection Glr., lóg-pa-mkań (log pa mkhan) a rebel Glr.; *lóg-pa-ċaṅ* (log pa can) rebellious, seditious W. II. adj. reversed, inverted; irrational, wrong, lóg-pai (log pa'i) lam (lam), lam (lam) lóg-pa (log pa) Mil. a wrong way; lóg-pa-la (log pa la) źúgs-pa (zhugs pa) ('to rush into error, to turn to what is wrong?'), also euphemism for to fornicate Stg.; lta-(ba)- log(-pa) (lta (ba) log (pa)) v. ltá-ba (lta ba); ċos-lóg (chos log) a wrong faith, false doctrine, heresy; grwa-lóg (grwa log), ǰo-lóg (jo log) col an apostate monk or nun; lóg - par (log par) and (col.) log (log) adv. wrong, amiss, erroneously, lóg-par (log par) sém-pa (sem pa) to think evil, to have suspicions (about a thing), often = lta-lóg (lta log) skyéd-pa (skyed pa) to sin; frq.: *log (log) ºdréṅ-pa* ('dren pa) to mislead, seduce B.; *log (log) yóṅ-ċe* (yong ce) W. to come back, to return, *ši-lóg (shi log) yóṅ-ċe* (yong ce) to recover life, to revive (after having been nearly lifeless), to rise from the dead, prob. also: to appear as a ghost W.; *nad (nad) log-gyáb (log rgyab) taṅ* (tang) W. the disease has become worse again, there has been a relapse; *la-lóg (la log) (bla-lóg (bla log)) pó-ċe* (pho ce) W. to turn, e.g. the roast; *ċud (cud) log (log) taṅ-ċe* (tang ce) v. yċud (gcud). Comp. log-ċos (log chos) Ma. = ċos-lóg (chos log). — log-rtógs (log rtogs) wrong judgment, false knowledge. — lóg-lta (log lta) = lta-lóg (lta log), v. ltá-ba (lta ba) — log-spyod (log spyod), Lt.: lóg-spyod (log spyod) ṅán-pa (ngan pa) perverse conduct, a sinful life. — lóg-ºtso (log 'tsho) with sgrúb-pa (sgrub pa) to live in a sinful manner, as much as: to live by crime, by vice Mil.

1. side, rtsíg-logs (rtsig logs) the side of a wall, mdún-logs (mdun logs) fore-side, front-side, rgyáb-logs (rgyab logs) back, back part of a thing; lógs-re (logs re) 1. side-post of a door (opp. to yá- (ya) and má-re (ma re)). 2. each side (v. re (re) 3); logs-bzáṅ (logs bzang) the right or upper side, logs-ṅán (logs ngan) the left or lower side (of a cloth) Cs.; surface, sai (sa'i) of the earth; side, direction, region, rkáṅ-pai-lógs-nas (rkang pa'i logs nas) from the part of the feet, up from the feet (e.g. a pain in the body proceeding up from the feet) Sch.; yyas-logs (g.yas logs) the right side, yyóṅ-logs (g.yon logs) the left side, frq.; tsú(r)-logs (tshu (r) logs) this side, on this side, ṕá(r)-logs (pha (r) logs) the other side, on the other side; lógs-su (logs su), lógs-la (logs la) aside, apart, *żág-pa* (zhag pa) C. to lay by, to put aside, to put out of the way, to clear away, lógs-su (logs su) dgár-ba (dgar ba), bkár-ba (bkar ba) means about the same; lógs-su (logs su) bkál-ba (bkal ba) to hang aside, to hang up in another place; lógs-na (logs na) yód-pa (yod pa) to be distinct, separate, to live by one's self, solitarily Schf., Tar. 45, 18; lógs-pa (logs pa) other, additional, by-, co-, spare-, rgyags (rgyags) logs-pa (logs pa) spare-provision, so also logs (logs) yċig (gcig): tág-pai (thag pa'i) sné-mo (sne mo) logs (logs) yċig (gcig) the other end of a rope. — 2. wall, *log-żál* (log zhal) W. id.; logs-brís (logs bris) mural or fresco painting Tar.

1. leisure, spare-time, vacant time, time, loṅ (long) yód-du (yod du) ré-la (re la) nám-zla (nam zla) ºdas ('das) whilst you are always hoping to have (still) time (enough), you allow the favourable moment to pass away Mil.; similarly: loṅ (long) yod (yod) snyam-la (snyam la) mi-tse (mi tshe) zad (zad) Mil.; sdód-pai (sdod pa'i) loṅ (long) méd-par (med par) without delay, immediately, directly Glr.; *ṅa (nga) yóṅ-loṅ (yong long) med* (med) C., W. I have not time to come; *ṕéb-loṅ (pheb long) yód-na* (yod na) if your honour have time to come C., W.; rdég-loṅ (rdeg long) yóṅ-bas (yong bas) as there will be yet plenty of time to beat (me, you had better hear me now) Mil.; loṅ-ytam (long gtam) Sch.: 'cheerful talk, animated conversation'. — 2. imp. of laṅ-ba (lang ba) and leṅ-pa (leng pa).

wylie: long ka, long kha, long ga
english: Med. intestines, entrails, guts; strictly taken it is said to denote only the blind gut (?); yár-'oṅ (yar 'ong), már-loṅ (mar long) Cs.: the upper gut, the lower gut or thin guts, thick guts; loṅ-nád (long nad) a disease of the guts.

1. pf and secondary form of ldoṅ-ba (ldong ba), as vb.: to be blind, and fig.: to be infatuated; as adj.: blind, blinded etc., as sbst.: blind man Dzl. — loṅ-krid (long khrid) (or ºkrid-pa ('khrid pa)) the guide of a blind man Lex.; lóṅ-po (long po), lóṅ-ba-po (long ba po) a blind man Cs.; *nye-lóṅ* (nye long) W. an empty ear of corn, a tare. — 2. also loṅs-pa (longs pa), = léṅ-pa (leng pa) Glr. or laṅ-ba (lang ba) 1, 2 Glr.
Stg., lóṅ-mo (long mo) Mil. ankle-bone, astragal.
